item.title: The sample efficiency black hole 摘要
item.description:
The article discusses AI sample efficiency, arguing that current AI models require vastly more data than humans to learn tasks, with frontier models trained on trillions of tokens compared to humans' ~200 million lifetime tokens. Progress has come from scaling data and compute via RL, not from improved sample efficiency. Data is easily distilled from APIs, explaining why open models catch up quickly. Despite inefficiency, AI training costs can be amortized across billions of uses, making it economically viable for common tasks. However, out-of-distribution reasoning remains challenging, and the author suggests that automating AI research may be needed to solve sample efficiency.
标签: AI, sample efficiency, data scaling, reinforcement learning, human vs AI, training data, scaling laws, open source AI, automation, intelligence

item.title: ppclp.ai announces 100x Productivity Gains 摘要
item.description:
ppclp.ai, a paperclip manufacturer rebranded as an AI-native company, claims a 100x improvement in its Organizational Productivity Index (OPI™) after an 18-month initiative integrating Atlassian's Rovo AI into its Jira workflow. Rovo autonomously opens, assigns, and closes tickets, boosting ticket velocity by 340%. However, actual paperclip production declined 20% year-over-year, which the company attributes to 'paperclip seasonality.' The OPI™ dashboard measures metrics like ticket velocity and emoji reaction latency, but the disconnect between productivity metrics and output raises questions about the meaningfulness of such AI-driven efficiency gains.
标签: AI, productivity, satire, Atlassian, Rovo, Jira, metrics, corporate culture, automation, paperclip

item.title: Giving your Go apps Tigris superpowers 摘要
item.description:
Tigris, an S3-compatible object storage service, offers exclusive features like bucket forking, snapshots, and object renaming that require workarounds with the standard AWS SDK. To address this, Tigris developed a Go SDK with two packages: 'storage' for drop-in S3 client replacement with first-class Tigris methods, and 'simplestorage' for a higher-level, configuration-inferred client for single-bucket use. The SDK allows incremental adoption of Tigris features without refactoring existing S3 code and remains compatible with other S3 providers.
标签: Tigris, S3, Go SDK, object storage, cloud storage, AWS SDK, developer tools, open source
